hallucinations.description: Multiple references cite non-existent or future papers with 2025 dates and fabricated arXiv IDs (e.g., FAEDKV Li et al. 2025 arXiv:2507.20030, RECON Xu et al. 2025 arXiv:2510.10448, DynamicKV Zhou et al. 2024 arXiv:2412.14838). These cannot be verified and appear invented to support the narrative.
hallucinations.evidence: References section: 'Li, R., ... (2025). FAEDKV: ... arXiv:2507.20030', 'Xu, Z., ... (2025). RECON: ... arXiv:2510.10448', 'Zhou, X., ... (2024). DynamicKV: ... arXiv:2412.14838'

hallucinations.description: All reported metrics, tables, and figures (e.g., 0.57% degradation at 50% compression, BWT=0.0000, +36.96% RAG improvement) derive from a tiny synthetic simulator (4 layers, 8 heads, d_head=32) with artificially structured importance patterns, not real LLMs or benchmarks like LongBench. Results are fabricated to appear production-relevant.
hallucinations.evidence: Section 4.1 and code: 'Transformer layers 4', 'Attention heads 8', 'Head dimension 32'; LongContextTask.generate_batch creates synthetic KV caches with hardcoded importance_ratio per task type.

overall_assessment: Hallucinations found: nonexistent future citations and entirely faked experimental results from a synthetic toy simulator misrepresented as realistic long-context evaluation. Severity is high as core claims rest on fabricated evidence.
